teachingPoint: T681 ArchitectureHistoryIngester reads XIIGen's own git history (commits, PRs, design docs) to extract architectural patterns and decisions. Every git commit, merged PR, and architecture doc is a data point: 'commit message mentions FLOW-08 Task Type T67', 'PR #123 updated DNA-8 implementation'. This creates a searchable history of architectural decisions without manual annotation.
positiveExample: T681: Parse git log, extract commit messages with FLOW-XX patterns. Parse merged PRs, extract linked Jira issues (DEV-123). Parse architecture docs, extract design decisions. All → xiigen-architecture-history index.
negativeExample: T681: Manual tagging of architectural decisions. Requires developers to annotate. Inconsistent, incomplete coverage.
discriminatingConstraint: T681 MUST extract architectural signal from git history automatically. No manual annotation required.
keywords: ArchitectureHistoryIngester git history extraction architectural decisions automated

teachingPoint: T682 DecisionExtractor identifies key architectural decisions from git history: when a design pattern (e.g., 'idempotency key') is introduced, changed, or removed. Patterns are often mentioned in commit messages ('feat: idempotency key for T68') or PR descriptions. The extractor builds a timeline of when each pattern entered the codebase and how it evolved.
positiveExample: T682: Commit 'DEV-8 feat(T68): idempotent registration via hash(userId,eventId,tenantId)' → extract (pattern:'idempotency_hash', decision:'use userId+eventId+tenantId', when:commitDate). Timeline of pattern evolution.
negativeExample: T682: Pattern is introduced, changed later, but no record of the evolution. Hard to understand why the pattern is the way it is.
discriminatingConstraint: T682 MUST extract decision timeline (when pattern introduced, changed, removed). Commit date and message are sources of truth.
keywords: DecisionExtractor decision timeline pattern evolution architectural history

teachingPoint: T683 DnaComplianceHistoryBuilder tracks how XIIGen has implemented and updated the 9 DNA patterns (DNA-1 through DNA-9) over time. For example: DNA-8 (outbox pattern) was implemented in FLOW-08, then strengthened in FLOW-12, then formalized in FLOW-PREREQ-01. The builder creates a timeline of DNA compliance: which flows implement which DNA, what version of each DNA pattern.
positiveExample: T683: FLOW-08 implements DNA-8 (storeDocument before enqueue). Later, FLOW-12 re-implements DNA-8 with same rule. FLOW-PREREQ-01 formalizes as BFA rule. Timeline shows convergence on the pattern.
negativeExample: T683: Each flow implements DNA-8 differently. No visibility into whether pattern is converging or diverging across flows.
discriminatingConstraint: T683 MUST track which flows implement which DNA patterns, with pattern version and compliance level. This enables 'drift detection' downstream.
keywords: DnaComplianceHistoryBuilder DNA pattern implementation tracking compliance timeline

teachingPoint: T684 BfaRuleEvolutionTracker tracks BFA rule history: when rules are introduced, modified, or deprecated. For example, CF-008-2 ('idempotency key must be business identity') was introduced in FLOW-08, then refined in FLOW-PREREQ-01. The tracker builds a graph of rule dependencies and changes: which BFA rules depend on which DNA patterns, which rules supersede earlier rules.
positiveExample: T684: CF-008-2 introduced in DEV-8 commit. Later, DEV-115 updates CF-008-2 with clarification. Tracker records both versions, shows lineage.
negativeExample: T684: BFA rules created ad-hoc, no historical record of when or why. Later developer guesses at rule intent.
discriminatingConstraint: T684 MUST track BFA rule versions, introduction date, and superseding rules. No deletion of historical records.
keywords: BfaRuleEvolutionTracker BFA rule history version tracking superseding rules

teachingPoint: T685 ConventionChangeDetector identifies when XIIGen's own conventions change (e.g., naming schemes, file structure, artifact numbering). For example, when FLOW-15 was released, task types changed from T221-T224 to T605-T608 (remapping). The detector logs convention changes: what changed, when, which flows are affected. This enables 'drift detection' (are we following our own rules?).
positiveExample: T685: Detects 'task type numbering changed from sequential to remapped' in DEV-115 commit. Logs affected flows (FLOW-15..24), the remapping rule. Enables validation that newer flows follow the new convention.
negativeExample: T685: No tracking of convention changes. Later developer introduces new convention, unaware of existing rules.
discriminatingConstraint: T685 MUST detect convention changes from git history and artifact naming. Changes logged with affected flows and rationale.
keywords: ConventionChangeDetector convention change detection naming schema remapping

teachingPoint: T686 ArchitectureBootstrapSynthesizer combines all history signals (architecture decisions, DNA compliance timeline, BFA rule evolution, convention changes) into a unified view of XIIGen's own architecture. This 'architecture self-portrait' is stored in xiigen-architecture-manifest. Later, when FLOW-PREREQ-01 (SpecAuditor) runs, it compares current state against this manifest to detect drift (new DNA patterns being violated, new conventions ignored, new rules conflicting).
positiveExample: T686: Reads xiigen-architecture-history, xiigen-dna-compliance, xiigen-bfa-rules, xiigen-conventions. Synthesizes unified manifest: 'These are the 9 DNA patterns we implement, these are the 100+ BFA rules we enforce, these are our naming conventions'. Stores in xiigen-architecture-manifest.
negativeExample: T686: No unified view. Each downstream system (SpecAuditor, BFA validator) reimplements architecture rules independently. Inconsistency.
discriminatingConstraint: T686 MUST produce xiigen-architecture-manifest with complete architecture specification (DNA, BFA, conventions). This is source of truth for drift detection.
keywords: ArchitectureBootstrapSynthesizer architecture manifest unified view self-portrait drift
